title: carex capillaris subsp. ledebouriana
intro: Carex capillaris subsp. ledebouriana, commonly known as the hare's foot sedge, is a subspecies of grass-like perennial plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Carex capillaris subsp. ledebouriana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
